mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed +1% in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315, inflationary pressures trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches the 315 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and unemployment decline signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 40% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
